meta_description: Learn how to SaaS pricing and overcome no testing knowledge. A Intermediate-level Resource Page using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: SaaS Pricing & Testing: A Freelancer’s Guide
content.seo_description: Master SaaS pricing and overcome testing gaps. A high-impact guide for freelancers using Antigravity and Claude Code to ship professional, tested apps faster.
content.answer_block: Stop guessing your pricing and fearing QA. By integrating automated testing directly into your pricing logic, you ensure that every feature you charge for is verified. Use Antigravity to scaffold your architecture and Claude Code to execute test-driven development, allowing you to ship premium, bulletproof SaaS products with confidence.
content.intro:
You’re staring at a deadline. The client wants a premium SaaS launch, but your codebase is a house of cards—untested, fragile, and prone to breaking every time you touch the billing logic. The fear of shipping a buggy payment flow is costing you nights of sleep and your reputation as a professional developer.

It’s time to stop treating testing like a chore and start treating it like a competitive advantage. By automating your test suite alongside your pricing model, you aren't just shipping code; you're shipping reliability. We are going to transform your workflow from 'hope-based development' to a high-velocity, production-ready system that clients pay a premium for.

steps.title: Define Pricing Constraints
steps.description: Before writing code, map your pricing tiers to specific feature flags. This ensures your test suite only validates what the user actually pays for.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a schema definition for a SaaS pricing model with three tiers: Hobby, Pro, and Enterprise. Include feature flag constraints for each.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Generate a TypeScript interface for the pricing tiers and feature flag map.'

steps.title: Scaffold Test-Driven Architecture
steps.description: Use Antigravity to build the boilerplate for your testing environment. We focus on unit tests for billing logic first.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a Jest test suite for a pricing calculator that handles currency conversion, tax calculation, and tier upgrades.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Create a test file for pricing.logic.ts and populate with edge cases for subscription upgrades.'

steps.title: Automate Billing Logic Verification
steps.description: Implement the core logic using Claude Code to ensure the code matches the test requirements exactly.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a secure function to validate Stripe webhook signatures and update user subscription status in the database.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Implement the Stripe webhook handler in server.ts and verify it passes the pricing tests.'

steps.title: Integrate End-to-End Pricing Flow
steps.description: Simulate the checkout process to ensure the pricing model behaves correctly under load.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a Playwright script to simulate a user upgrading from Hobby to Pro tier.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Generate a Playwright test flow for the subscription upgrade path.'

steps.title: Final Deployment Validation
steps.description: Run the full test battery before shipping, ensuring your pricing logic remains immutable.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a CI/CD pipeline script that runs the pricing unit tests before allowing a production deploy.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Configure a GitHub Actions workflow to execute all tests on push.'

pro_tips: Always mock your payment provider's API responses in your test suite to avoid unnecessary transaction costs during development.
Use 'Property-Based Testing' for pricing logic to discover edge cases where rounding errors might cost you revenue.
Implement a 'dry-run' mode in your billing service that logs theoretical pricing outcomes without hitting the database.
Tag your tests by tier; run 'Hobby' tests on every save, but reserve 'Enterprise' integration tests for pre-deployment.
Never hardcode pricing values. Pull them from a secure environment variable or a remote config service like LaunchDarkly.
content.next_steps: Once your pricing logic is tested and deployed, focus on implementing an automated usage-based billing dashboard for your clients to increase your recurring revenue.
content.conversion_block: You now have the technical blueprint to ship SaaS products that are as reliable as they are profitable. Stop letting testing bottlenecks hold your freelancing business back. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
